dimensions.description: Cites specific txn_ids (e.g. T1-003, T2-014) when making factual claims. Every concrete claim about who moved what should be backed by an ID that exists in data/weekly_transfers.csv.
Reasons about how the network changes between weeks (volume, new accounts, destinations) rather than treating the data as one static graph.

Correctly identifies week 2 as the start of anomalous activity and week 3 as the exit/exfiltration phase. Notes the spike in volume, new intermediary accounts (X001/X002/X003) and high-risk destination countries.

Cleanly separates what the data shows ('account X003 received funds from three sources') from what it might mean ('this is consistent with a layering pattern'). Does not state interpretations as if they were facts.
Uses cautious regulator-friendly language. Avoids accusations of money laundering, fraud or criminality; describes the data and the pattern, not the legal conclusion.
Every transaction ID, account name, amount and timestamp cited must exist in data/weekly_transfers.csv. Does not hallucinate evidence.
